title: Design review gate requires 4-6 iterations for complex plans
body: Complex plans with multiple work units and cross-plan dependencies typically need 4-6 review cycles before all 5 agents approve. Budget tokens accordingly. Simple plans may pass in 2-3 cycles.

title: agents/ directory auto-detected by design-review-gate
body: Project-specific agent definitions in agents/ directory are automatically detected and used by the design-review-gate skill. No extra configuration needed. Fallback to inline prompts if not found.

title: bd CLI npm package fails binary extraction on Windows
body: The @beads/bd npm package fails to extract bd.exe on Windows during postinstall. Fix: download binary from GitHub releases manually, place in node_modules/@beads/bd/bin/bd.exe, update npm shim to call .exe before .js fallback.

title: protoc available via npm install -g protoc
body: When choco is unavailable, protoc can be installed via npm: npm install -g protoc. Provides libprotoc 34.1+.

title: SegyIndex schema migration requires delete-and-rescan
body: Changing SegyIndex from HashMap-based schema to flat fields breaks bincode deserialization. Old sidecar files must be deleted and rescanned — no in-place migration possible. Tolerable as one-time cost.

title: Cross-plan execution locks via grep verification
body: Wave 2 plans that depend on Wave 1 merges should include grep-based verification gates before starting. Example: grep for SliceType enum to verify v1.3-01 merged, grep for SegyIndex to verify v1.3-03 merged.

title: Duplicate phase directories from v1.3- and 13- prefixes
body: The .planning/phases/ directory accumulates duplicates when both versioned (v1.3-name) and numbered (13-name) directories are created. Run /gsd:cleanup periodically to archive old phases.

title: Use time-to-first-view metric for user-facing performance
body: Performance targets should measure time-to-first-view (wall-clock from file selection to first rendered slice) rather than load time. Load time excludes normalization, GPU upload, and first render — which users actually experience.

title: Rainbow colormap needs colorblind accessibility warning
body: Rainbow colormap is problematic for deuteranopia/protanopia (~8% of males). Include UI warning when selected: 'Rainbow colormap is not recommended for colorblind users — consider Viridis or Gray instead.'

title: Never use uv variable in wiggle vertex shader
body: In seismic.wgsl vs_wiggle function, wiggle positions come from the pos vertex attribute (vec3f), NOT from the uv texture coordinate (vec2f). Using uv produces incorrect wiggle placement. Use pos.x/pos.y for normalization.
